The Complete Guide to SEO in 2025

Search engine optimization has changed dramatically in the past few years. With the rise of AI-powered search engines, traditional SEO alone is no longer enough.

What is SEO?

SEO stands for Search Engine Optimization. It is the practice of improving a website to increase its visibility in search engine results pages.

What is GEO?

GEO is Generative Engine Optimization — the practice of optimizing content to be cited by AI-powered search engines like ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Google AI Overviews.

Technical SEO Fundamentals

Technical SEO covers the infrastructure of your website. This includes site speed, mobile-friendliness, crawlability, and structured data.

Core Web Vitals

Core Web Vitals are a set of metrics that measure the user experience of a page. They include Largest Contentful Paint, Interaction to Next Paint, and Cumulative Layout Shift.

Frequently Asked Questions

Below are the most common questions we get about SEO and GEO.

What is SEO?

SEO stands for Search Engine Optimization. It is the process of improving a website to increase its organic visibility in search engine results pages. Good SEO combines technical excellence, high-quality content, and authoritative backlinks.

How long does SEO take?

SEO typically takes 3-6 months to show meaningful results. This is because search engines need time to crawl, index, and evaluate the quality of your pages. Technical fixes can appear faster, while content authority and link building take longer to accumulate.

GEO: The Next Frontier

Generative Engine Optimization is the practice of ensuring your content is cited by AI-powered tools like ChatGPT, Perplexity, Claude, and Google AI Overviews. The core principle is simple: if your content ranks well in traditional search, it is already eligible for AI citation. No special markup or separate optimization track is required for Google AI Overviews specifically.

However, specific signals increase your likelihood of being cited. These include providing direct, factual answers early in each section, using FAQ schema markup, naming proprietary frameworks or concepts, and including original data or statistics. Bing officially added GEO to its Webmaster Guidelines in 2024, making it the first major search engine to formally define the discipline.

Internal Linking Strategy

A strong internal linking structure helps search engines understand the topical hierarchy of your site. The best approach is to create a pillar page for each core topic, then link all related cluster pages back to the pillar. Use descriptive anchor text that matches the target page's primary keyword. Avoid generic anchor text like "click here" or "read more" — these provide no context to crawlers or users.
